name: Archimedean Spiral
description: A frequency-independent spiral antenna where the radius increases linearly with angle, providing broadband operation with circular polarization. The self-complementary structure maintains constant impedance and radiation characteristics over multiple octaves. Widely used in applications requiring ultra-wideband performance with circular polarization.

outer_radius.name: Outer Radius
outer_radius.description: Maximum radius of the spiral determining the lowest operating frequency

inner_radius.name: Inner Radius
inner_radius.description: Minimum radius of the spiral determining the highest operating frequency

growth_rate.name: Growth Rate
growth_rate.description: Rate at which radius increases with angle in Archimedean spiral equation r = a * phi

num_turns.name: Number of Turns
num_turns.description: Total number of spiral turns from inner to outer radius

arm_width.name: Arm Width
arm_width.description: Width of the spiral conductor arms

arm_spacing.name: Arm Spacing
arm_spacing.description: Spacing between spiral arms for self-complementary design

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining spiral dimensions based on frequency requirements, ensuring self-complementary geometry for constant impedance, and optimizing the feeding structure for broadband matching.

steps.title: Frequency Range Specification
steps.description: Define the required operating frequency range and calculate corresponding wavelengths
steps.formula: lambda_min = c / f_min, lambda_max = c / f_max

steps.title: Outer Radius Calculation
steps.description: Calculate outer radius based on lowest frequency for proper radiation

steps.title: Inner Radius Calculation
steps.description: Calculate inner radius based on highest frequency and practical constraints

steps.title: Growth Rate Determination
steps.description: Calculate the growth rate parameter for the Archimedean spiral

steps.title: Self-Complementary Design
steps.description: Set arm width and spacing equal for self-complementary structure

steps.title: Impedance Matching
steps.description: Design balun or feeding structure for 50-ohm impedance matching

steps.title: Substrate Selection
steps.description: Choose substrate thickness and dielectric constant for optimal performance

equations.resonantFrequency: f = c / (2 * pi * r) for each radius point

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use self-complementary theory with Z0 = 188.5 ohms, apply balun transformation to 50 ohms
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Frequency-independent pattern with circular polarization, use active region concept at circumference = wavelength

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 14
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 12
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 14
Kraus, J.D. 'Antennas for All Applications', 3rd Edition, Chapter 13
